title: Implement Zephyr /msg or local message send contract for app ingress
description: Why this exists: docs describe Meshtastic TEXT_MESSAGE_APP as a CoAP /msg or /msg/inbox send, but the Zephyr C gateway currently has only /status, /neighbors, and /config resources plus a Meshtastic adapter callback boundary. What needs to be done: define the concrete Zephyr local message ingress API/resource, connect Meshtastic broadcast text callback to it, and document the selected path. Acceptance criteria: no-hardware test observes the message contract call or CoAP request; unsupported/no-route failures are deterministic; no LoRa/RF send path is invoked unless the normal LICHEN stack owns it.
notes: Implementation evidence 2026-07-01: added gateway message_contract module as the production lichen_app_interface.submit_text provider for compatibility app ingress. The contract copies text events, including has_to_iid/to_iid, into a bounded local queue; returns -ENOMEM for full queue backpressure; exposes gateway_message_contract_pop_text() for the normal LICHEN sender; and does not transmit on BLE or LoRa itself. Gateway startup now initializes the contract before Meshtastic/MeshCore compatibility adapters. Meshtastic gateway tests now observe the production contract queue for known directed text and deterministic local queue backpressure. Reviews: Einstein and Sagan found no P0/P1/P2; P3 follow-ups filed as project-LICHEN-t2hn.7.2.1, project-LICHEN-t2hn.7.2.2, and project-LICHEN-t2hn.7.3. close_reason: Completed no-hardware implementation: gateway message_contract now provides the production submit_text sink, copies Meshtastic directed IID metadata into a bounded local queue, backpressures deterministically, and has native_sim/qemu/build evidence recorded in notes.
